{{Quote|…he's real enough. And everything you've heard about him is true.|Han Solo, describing Phelon|<ref name="Star Wars Missions 15: Voyage to the Underworld">''[[Star Wars Missions 15: Voyage to the Underworld]]''</ref>}}
'''Phelon''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Columi/Legends|Columi]] who resided on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Despite being a creature of diminutive stature, his head was so large that it required [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] assistance to stay upright.

Rumors abounded about Phelon; the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] claimed that the Columi had been executed, but others thought him to be either a criminal mastermind, a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] sympathizer, or a secret Imperial agent. Either way, he was thought to be very powerful, and had an inexhaustible supply of surveillance data.

On the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Columus/Legends|Columus]], Phelon ran into trouble and, through the help of [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], relocated to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Solo faked Phelon's death and smuggled him out, essentially erasing evidence of his continued existence. Following his relocation, Phelon lived in a [[Castle/Legends|castle]] dating back to the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], deep in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]] on the shore of a lake of burning [[Water/Legends|water]]. Phelon's castle was protected by his domesticated [[giant walking fish]], [[Hobbrock]]. Buried in the bowels of Coruscant, Phelon constantly monitored [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] communications, especially data dealing with finances and security. In addition, in his false role as an engineering consultant for dummy corporations of his own design, he managed to indirectly siphon Imperial credits meant for mining and [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to the cause of the Alliance to Restore the Republic.

Sometime shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Solo and his companions—[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]—became trapped on Coruscant through the scheming [[Computer/Legends|computer]] virus of [[B-1 series protocol droid]] [[B-1D4]]. The gang resorted to seek out Phelon, who repaid his long-standing debt to Solo by infecting the Imperial military's security network with B-1D4's virus, thus allowing the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and her crew to escape.
